title: A figure in the window
premise: False encounter: the survivor who isn't.

stages.narration: Across the courtyard, a third-floor window: a figure standing at the glass, one arm half-raised, not moving. You raise a hand back before you can stop yourself. It doesn't wave. It doesn't do anything.

choices.label: Go up and check — someone might be alive up there
You know that stillness by now — leave it
Call out to them, once, low

effects.note: called up to the figure in the window; nothing answered

notes: A body, weeks dead, propped upright by a fallen coat-rack and the draft. No payoff — the point is the hope and its cost. Three approaches (approach/leave/call) all resolve to nothing — FR-CNT-04 Rule of Three honoured without inventing a payoff; calling costs real noise.
